signals.idea: Harden the Solana payment subsystem against the attack vectors found by the adversarial security review. Fix P0: git history secret audit, prompt-injection hardening in admin_chat (remove LLM-sourced wallet addresses from initiate_payment tool; wallets must come from wallet_registry keyed by mentioned Discord user), null recipient_discord_id confirmation bypass (fail-closed). Fix P1: wallet swap window between test and final payment (verify execute_payment uses stored recipient_wallet string; reject or warn on upsert_wallet during active intents), idempotency slot reuse after forced failure (warn/DM on producer_ref collision with prior failed row having different wallet), per-payment and rolling daily USD cap on admin-triggered payouts enforced in PaymentService (default $500/payment, $2000/day/guild, exceeding goes to manual_hold), admin DM on successful admin_chat payouts over threshold (default $100, differentiated from failure DMs). Fix P2: log audit for secret leakage, document RLS posture at top of sql/payments.sql. Already using Helius so RPC attacks out of scope. Two wallets already split into solana_grants and solana_payouts providers. Think carefully about wallet-swap and idempotency — they are subtle and easy to half-fix. Goal is end-to-end hardening.

unresolved_flags.concern: Solana payout caps: Step 8 still leaves admin-chat final payouts uncapped because it keys cap enforcement off `normalized_amount_usd`, while the live final-payout caller passes `amount_token` only.

Wallet registry updates: The proposed `upsert_wallet` blocked-path sentinel is only handled in admin chat, but `grants_cog` is another live caller and would treat a truthy sentinel dict as a successful wallet record.

Payment idempotency: The collision path over-assumes it can insert a new `manual_hold` row for any prior-wallet mismatch, but the active unique index still blocks inserts when the prior row is not `failed` or `cancelled`.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the required payout-cap item against the real admin payout caller in `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:352-365`; that path calls `request_payment(... amount_token=float(intent['requested_amount_sol']), amount_usd=None ...)`, but Step 8 in the plan says cap logic runs only when `normalized_amount_usd is not None`, so the plan as written does not fully cover the main admin-triggered payout path called out in the brief.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Step 5 says to add the active-payment guard in `upsert_wallet` before the existing-wallet lookup, but the sample code branches on `existing` before `existing = self.get_wallet(...)` has run. That control flow is not implementable as written and needs to be reordered or rewritten.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The collision path in Step 6 assumes it can create a fresh `manual_hold` row whenever any prior `(producer, producer_ref, is_test)` row has a different wallet, but `sql/payments.sql:126-128` still enforces uniqueness for every status except `failed` and `cancelled`. If the differing prior row is `pending_confirmation`, `queued`, `processing`, `submitted`, `confirmed`, or `manual_hold`, the insert cannot succeed, so the plan needs an explicit non-insert branch instead of assuming a new held row can always be created.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: `upsert_wallet` is shared outside admin chat: `src/features/grants/grants_cog.py:557-603` also uses it during grant wallet verification. The plan only describes surfacing the blocked-update path in `admin_chat_cog._handle_wallet_received`, so the broader caller surface is not fully accounted for.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The plan says to grep for `PaymentCog(` and find a single construction site, but there are two repo instantiations today: `main.py:233` and `src/features/payments/payment_cog.py:503`. If the constructor grows success-DM configuration, both locations need to stay in sync.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The supporting test glue is broader than the plan currently describes. The fake databases and bot doubles that would need to absorb helper or return-shape changes live inline in `tests/test_admin_payments.py` and `tests/test_social_publish_service.py`, not only in `tests/conftest.py`.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the real admin payout caller at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:352-365`; it passes `amount_token` and omits `amount_usd`, so any cap logic written around the grants-style USD caller alone will miss the exact caller the brief cares about most.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked `upsert_wallet` callers and found both `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:193-201` and `src/features/grants/grants_cog.py:557-562`. A truthy sentinel such as `{'__error__': ...}` would be caught in admin chat only if the new branch is added there, but grants would still treat that dict as a valid wallet record and continue.

Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Several `must` criteria are not directly verifiable from code, tests, or diff inspection alone, especially the requirements that the git-history audit commands and log-audit grep be run and that their findings be recorded. Those are useful review tasks, but they are not strong `must` gates for an automated review pipeline.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 19.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 12.

items.criterion: All existing tests in tests/test_admin_payments.py, tests/test_scheduler.py, and tests/test_social_publish_service.py still pass unchanged (default None caps preserve existing PaymentService construction).
New test test_confirm_rejects_null_recipient_discord_id passes: confirm_payment with recipient_discord_id=None returns None and logs WARN.
New test verifying privileged_override=True still bypasses null-recipient check passes.
New test test_confirm_rejects_mismatched_user passes: confirm_payment with a non-matching confirmed_by_user_id returns None.
New test test_request_payment_per_payment_cap_manual_holds passes: amount_usd over per-payment cap lands the row as manual_hold with cap-breach last_error; grants provider is unaffected.

New test test_request_payment_rolling_daily_cap_manual_holds passes: rolling 24h aggregation across non-test solana_payouts rows in (pending_confirmation, queued, processing, submitted, confirmed) drives the cap; grants are unaffected.
New test test_slot_reuse_collision_detected passes: prior row with different recipient_wallet triggers manual_hold creation plus admin DM, NOT silent replacement.

New test test_slot_reuse_same_wallet_creates_fresh_row_after_failure passes: when the only prior rows are terminal and the wallet matches, a new pending_confirmation row is created (pins reordered early-return).
New test test_idempotent_return_for_nonterminal passes: non-terminal prior row is returned unchanged.

New test test_upsert_wallet_blocked_during_active_intent passes: upsert_wallet refuses updates while a non-terminal payment_request or active admin_payment_intent exists and _handle_wallet_received surfaces the error.
New test test_upsert_wallet_unblocked_after_terminal passes: upsert proceeds when only terminal rows exist.

New test test_admin_success_dm_over_threshold passes: confirmed, non-test, solana_payouts, amount_usd >= threshold triggers _dm_admin_payment_success; under-threshold does not; failure DM path is unchanged.
New test test_execute_payment_uses_stored_wallet passes: execute_payment passes payment['recipient_wallet'] to provider.send even when wallet_registry has been updated (regression pin).
New test test_initiate_payment_tool_rejects_wallet_address_arg passes: wallet_address key in params is popped and ignored; wallet is resolved exclusively via db_handler.get_wallet.
PaymentService constructor accepts per_payment_usd_cap, daily_usd_cap, capped_providers kwargs; all default to None so existing construction sites remain compatible.
Cap enforcement in PaymentService runs only in request_payment, never in execute_payment; scheduler path (claim_due_payment_requests -> execute_payment) is untouched.
Cap-breach, collision, and null-recipient paths create a manual_hold row (or reject for null) AND trigger an admin DM; no silent rejection path for these failure modes.
_dm_admin_payment_success is a new sibling method with distinct prefix/emoji from _dm_admin_payment_failure; _dm_admin_payment_failure itself is unchanged.
main.py wires per_payment_usd_cap, daily_usd_cap, capped_providers=('solana_payouts',), and admin_success_dm_threshold from env vars with defaults $500/$2000/$100.

upsert_wallet blocks the update only when the incoming address differs from the stored address AND an active payment/intent exists; identical-address upserts and upserts without active intents remain unblocked.
sql/payments.sql has a top-of-file comment block documenting the RLS posture (RLS already enabled, anon/authenticated revoked, service-role bypass); no ALTER TABLE changes.
Git history secret audit commands from Step 3 are run and findings recorded in the review output (even if empty).
Log audit grep from Step 11 is performed and findings recorded. If no real leak path found, no log_handler.py code change.
Admin success DM is provider-scoped to capped_providers (solana_payouts) and threshold-gated; solana_grants payouts never trigger admin success DMs.
pytest tests/ runs green end-to-end.
New cap/collision/DM code paths redact wallets via existing _redact_wallet helpers and never log env-var values or key material.

has_active_payment_or_intent uses the same non-terminal predicate ('completed','failed','cancelled' for intents; 'confirmed','failed','manual_hold','cancelled' for payments) as existing helpers — no ad-hoc drift.
Inline comment at the collision-detection + idempotent-return reorder explains WHY terminal rows must not short-circuit the early return.
Rolling-24h cap helper includes a comment documenting the residual burst-race window under non-serializable Supabase REST access.
Plan output explicitly states that the P0 prompt-injection premise was already substantially satisfied by prior work, to prevent the review agent from marking prep as incomplete.
No unrelated refactors or test rewrites beyond what the new behavior strictly requires.
